paragraphs.body: I read an article about the life cycle of stars. As I was reading, it felt familiar to a story I knew. I felt like I could relate to the loneliness and pain of the star as it fades away. Once so bright and majestic turns into something like a black hole. In the article “Stellar Evolution,” the author explains that stars initially form from a cloud of gas and dust called a nebula and go through various phases until they die. The phases of a small star include the main sequence, red giant, planetary nebula, white dwarf, and finally, black dwarf. Each of these phases reminded me of the happiness and struggles my generation faces in my country, leading me to think, why not write about them? The stars share the same destiny with Afghan girls. Once, they too arose from nothing, were burning brightly, and now are fading away. This is just one of a million stories. It is the story of Afghan girls.

annotations.text: The stars share the same destiny with Afghan girls. Once, they too arose from nothing, were burning brightly, and now are fading away.
I felt like I could relate to the loneliness and pain of the star as it fades away.
annotations.comment: Great moment of heat and energy! Let's talk about it!

paragraphs.body: Not long ago, a baby girl was born in a country known for its majestic mountains and harsh winters. They named her Bahar, which means spring in English. When a new star is born, it is a ball of gas brimming with energy fueled by fusion; similarly, she was full of potential and curiosity. She was bright and filled with dreams, knowledge, and purpose. She was destined to shine.

annotations.comment: I'm interested in the way you use images that could be in a graphic novel--there's something really compelling in that, but these images also sort of "remove" you and your experience/ideas from the essay. Let's talk!
annotations.context_left: uriosity. She was bright and filled with dreams, knowledge, and purpose. She was destined to shine.

paragraphs.body: As she grew, she was confused and wondered whether to love her country or hate it. She began noticing the harsh realities in her country. She realized there was a clear societal imbalance between girls and boys. She discovered that there are still stigmas surrounding girls' education. She recognized the many ridiculous cultural beliefs that made no sense. She understood that her eagerness for knowledge might come at the cost of her life. She had too many reasons to give up and never arise, but she also realized that her country was desperately in need of help. She was exhausted by witnessing and hearing the hardships and stories of her people. She was tired of seeing her peers working in the streets instead of playing. She did not want her people to suffer any longer, so she dared to dream. She was the warrior and was ready to fight till the end, with her only weapons being a pen and a book.

annotations.text: As she grew, she was confused and wondered whether to love her country or hate it. She began noticing the harsh realities in her country. She realized there was a clear societal imbalance between girls and boys. She discovered that there are still stigmas surrounding girls' education. She recognized the many ridiculous cultural beliefs that made no sense. She understood that her eagerness for knowledge might come at the cost of her life.
annotations.comment: The heat and energy is compelling and powerful throughout this paragraph. I wonder, though, why you are making up a girl rather than talking about your own family, self, and history? Let's talk :)

paragraphs.body: When a star forms, its strong gravity pulls in on itself. Since the star is a gas, it could collapse, but the pressure from nuclear fusion reactions deep in the star’s core prevents this. Just like that, all those imperfections and pressures became the source of fuel that kept her fighting and motivated her to chase her dreams. Those weaknesses and struggles fueled her determination to strive even harder. She wanted to be a change-maker. She wanted to be someone independent of others. She wanted to be her country's pride, and she knew the only way to transform those imperfections into perfection and change her country’s fate was through knowledge.

The star, in its main sequence phase, fuses hydrogen into helium. Hydrogen fuels the star, allowing it to shine brightly. If this process remains uninterrupted or if it does not run out of hydrogen, the star can evolve and live for billions of years. Just like that, she still had the fuel burning inside her. Despite all her struggles, her country had allowed her to fly. She could still see hope and was able to pursue her dreams. She was achieving what she wanted. In her mind, she kept building dreams and setting goals. She was shining brightly in her chaotic world, and the future looked radiant and promising as she got closer and closer. Until one day…

annotations.comment: I'm very interested in the metaphor you're creating, but I wonder if you need to make the connections for your reader so directly? I think your narrative already suggests the metaphor :)
annotations.context_left: tic world, and the future looked radiant and promising as she got closer and closer. Until one day…

paragraphs.body: When the hydrogen in the center of the star depletes, the star draws upon the hydrogen further from its core. This process causes the star to expand and become a red giant.

Once the star completely exhausts its fuel, it creates a planetary nebula and the outer layer dissipates, leaving only a white dwarf behind. The star is reduced to a mere white dwarf, no longer shining as brightly as it once did, signaling the end of its life. Similarly, all the lights and fuel of her life began to dim. Suddenly, another force took control of her country and turned her into complete darkness. They told her to stop dreaming and forget all the years she had worked for. They crushed and shattered her world of goals and success. They told her to be silent and act as if she didn’t exist. Her existence suddenly felt so shameful and unbearable. The very first place she called home now denies her and treats her like she is nothing. She is no longer allowed to shine. They confiscated her book and pen, restricting her freedom to go outside as she once did. They want her to be invisible. Yet, she refuses to give up. She still clings to her hopes and dreams. She fights against them for her rights. She cries out for her rights, but no one hears her. They aim to erase her existence. She is forced to obey and remain silent. Like the star nearing the end of its life, she is shrinking inward—still present but unheard.

annotations.text: Suddenly, another force took control of her country and turned her into complete darkness. They told her to stop dreaming and forget all the years she had worked for. They crushed and shattered her world of goals and success. They told her to be silent and act as if she didn’t exist.
annotations.comment: What is your personal relationship to this moment? How has your upbringing and your family's country impacted you?

annotations.text: leaving only a white dwarf behind

paragraphs.body: A white dwarf begins to cool slowly and transforms into a black dwarf. A black dwarf is simply a white dwarf that has cooled to the point of no longer emitting heat or light. She now resembles that black dwarf—silent, resigned to a fate she never desired. Once, she shone brightly, but now she is lost in eternal darkness. Yet she is not gone; like the remnants of a collapsed star, her existence has made an impression. The world may attempt to erase her, but her voice, like the echoes of the cosmos, refuses to fade away. She remains present, though invisible, unheard, and forgotten.

annotations.text: She remains present, though invisible, unheard, and forgotten.
